constraints: no credentials or auth material created

evidence: README.md:148-162 documents x.ai OAuth login with --xai-oauth-login, --no-browser, and --oauth-callback-port; cmd/server/main.go:127-132 registers the helper flags and --xai-oauth-login.
assertions.reason: English README describes x.ai as OAuth, not API-key auth, and uses the actual CLI flag plus relevant helper flags.

evidence: README.md:164-177 identifies xai-oauth, grok-4.3, oauth-model-alias, and oauth-excluded-models; config.example.yaml:481-512 includes xai-oauth/grok-4.3 examples.
assertions.reason: Docs and config examples cover x.ai OAuth routing/channel naming and model alias/exclusion configuration.

evidence: README.md:181-199 documents zai-api-key, https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4, GLM examples glm-5.1/glm-5/glm-5-turbo, and warns never to commit real Z.AI keys; config.example.yaml:203-216 mirrors the setup.
assertions.reason: Z.AI GLM Coding Plan API-key setup is documented with the Coding Plan URL, model examples, and credential safety guidance.

evidence: README.md:201-217 documents provider type zai, api_key/api-key and base_url/base-url support with placeholders; internal/store/zai_auth.go and sdk/auth/filestore.go grep hits show implemented metadata handling and default base URL behavior.
assertions.reason: Z.AI auth-file usage is documented with implemented fields only, placeholder credentials, and no observed real Z.AI secrets in docs/config evidence.

evidence: README.md:220-233 and config.example.yaml:368-377 show opencode-zen as openai-compatibility with https://opencode.ai/zen/v1, api-key-entries, <OPENCODE_API_KEY>, prefix zen, and alias zen-default.
assertions.reason: OpenCode Zen documentation is factual for the Zen setup and uses placeholder key text with Zen-specific routing.

evidence: README.md:220-245 and config.example.yaml:378-387 show opencode-go as openai-compatibility with https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1, api-key-entries, <OPENCODE_API_KEY>, prefix go, and alias go-default distinct from Zen.
assertions.reason: OpenCode Go documentation is factual for the Go setup and uses placeholder key text with Go-specific routing distinct from Zen.

evidence: README.md:222 explicitly states OpenCode Zen/Go use /chat/completions and do not cover /responses, WebSocket, or Anthropic /messages routes; scoped grep found no contradictory OpenCode /messages instructions.
assertions.reason: Docs explicitly limit the documented OpenCode path to OpenAI-compatible chat completions and exclude Anthropic /messages support.

evidence: README.md:102-108 explains local proxy api-keys versus upstream provider credentials and local /v1 base URL; README.md:162, 199, and 245 show provider-specific client usage through the local proxy.
assertions.reason: Docs clearly separate downstream local proxy API keys from upstream provider credentials and show local /v1 endpoint shapes.

evidence: Promotional/non-English grep found README.md:3 linking README_CN.md; README.md:11-37 sponsor/promotional content and brands PackyCode, AICodeMirror, BmoPlus, VisionCoder with discount/promo/invitecode/aff= terms.
assertions.reason: Repository docs are not English-only/non-promotional: README still includes a Chinese README link and sponsor/promotional sections/terms.

commandsRun.command: mkdir -p mission evidence and report directories
rg static evidence collection for x.ai, Z.AI, OpenCode, local proxy, and promo terms > static-grep-evidence.txt
commandsRun.observation: Collected snippets and grep observations into mission evidence file.
Local targeted tests passed; no live upstream calls or persistent service.
Whitespace check passed for current diff.

summary: Validated 9 auth documentation assertions: 8 passed and 1 failed (VAL-DOCS-009 due remaining non-English link and promotional sponsor content in README).
